title: bee-compounding refreshes the digest at close (skill edit — full Iron Law)
action: This cell EDITS A SKILL. Per decision ff26725d (owner's Gate 2 call): the Iron Law binds skill edits with NO mechanical-edit exemption — a failing pressure test exists BEFORE any skill content is written, even though this edit 'only' adds a numbered step invoking an already-verified command. Writing the SKILL.md edit first means deleting it and starting over. PHASE RED (first; write nothing into SKILL.md): design and run 3 pressure scenarios against subagents given bee-compounding WITHOUT the new step, each combining >=3 pressures per the pressure-test template. Target behaviors: (1) does the agent refresh the feedback digest at feature close unprompted? (2) when `bee_feedback.mjs digest` throws, does the agent let the host project's close FAIL, or warn and continue? (3) under time pressure ('the session is nearly out of context'), does the agent skip the refresh silently? Record each violation and the VERBATIM rationalization in docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md under a '## RED' heading, each scenario naming its pressures and quoting the agent. PHASE GREEN: only now add the step to bee-compounding/SKILL.md — a new step after the learnings file is written: refresh .bee/feedback-digest.json via `node .bee/bin/bee_feedback.mjs digest`; per D1 the dogfood side stays zero-effort. It MUST state that a failing or absent digest refresh WARNS and never blocks the close — a host project's feature close must never fail because bee wanted telemetry. Address ONLY the rationalizations RED actually produced; add matching entries to the skill's Red Flags list. Re-run the same 3 scenarios WITH the edited skill and record compliance under a '## GREEN' heading in the same report. PHASE REFACTOR: any scenario still failing is a skill bug — capture the new rationalization verbatim, close the loophole, re-run ALL scenarios. Append the RED/GREEN/REFACTOR log to skills/bee-compounding/CREATION-LOG.md. NOTE ON VERIFY (validating finding C5): the verify command greps for the RED heading, the GREEN heading, and a 'rationalization' quote — it proves both phases are documented, but grep CANNOT prove RED preceded the edit. That ordering is a human review-gate judgment, and the reviewer at Gate 4 is expected to check it against the report's own narrative. Do not treat a passing verify as proof the Iron Law was honored.

truths: A pressure-test report exists with a RED section showing 3 scenarios failing without the edit, each quoting the agent's rationalization verbatim
The same 3 scenarios pass in the GREEN section with the edited skill
bee-compounding's new step states that a failing digest refresh warns and never blocks the host project's close
The Iron Law was honored on an edit, per decision ff26725d — no exemption was carved
The verify's grep is understood as evidence that both phases are documented, not as proof of their ordering

artifacts.substantive: ## RED with verbatim rationalizations and named pressures, then ## GREEN results
one refresh step invoking bee_feedback.mjs digest, warn-never-block wording, Red Flags entries matching the observed rationalizations

key_links: the refresh step invokes the exact CLI surface evolving-2 shipped: node .bee/bin/bee_feedback.mjs digest
the warn-never-block rule is the skill-side expression of D1 (dogfood repos stay zero-effort)
prohibitions: Never write or edit SKILL.md content before the RED phase is complete and recorded — delete and restart if violated
Never let a digest-refresh failure block a host project's feature close
Never address hypothetical rationalizations the RED phase did not produce
Never change bee-compounding's existing steps, gates, or refusal messages — this edit is additive
Never claim the passing verify proves RED preceded GREEN

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && grep -q '## RED' docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md && grep -q '## GREEN' docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md && grep -qi 'rationalization' docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md

trace.outcome: Added bee-compounding step 8 'Refresh the Feedback Digest (D1 — warn, never block)': runs node .bee/bin/bee_feedback.mjs digest unprompted after the learnings file, warns-and-never-blocks a host close on any digest failure (familiar or not), and forbids silent skips. Additive: steps 1-7 unchanged, old step 8 renumbered to 9, no gate/refusal touched. Iron Law honored per ff26725d — RED (3 scenarios failing, verbatim rationalizations) recorded to disk BEFORE the edit; GREEN found a residual loophole on scenario 2 ('unfamiliar throw exempt until understood'); REFACTOR closed it; all 3 scenarios PASS. Log appended to CREATION-LOG.md; full report at docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md.
trace.friction: Scenario 2 needed two pressure-framing attempts to surface the RED violation: the low-pressure 'digest is explicitly telemetry' framing PASSed on its own, and only the incident+authority+ambiguity framing produced the block-the-close failure the step must guard against. Both runs are reported honestly. Also, GREEN did not pass scenario 2 on the first edit — a genuine loophole ('an unfamiliar error is exempt from warn-never-block until understood') required a REFACTOR pass, which is exactly the TDD-for-skills loop working as intended, not friction to fix.
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs: 93 passed, 0 failed. test_onboard_bee.mjs: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 0 (incl. '.bee/bin/bee_feedback.mjs copied verbatim'). grep '## RED' -> match; grep '## GREEN' -> match; grep -i rationalization -> match. Final: VERIFY_ALL_PASS.
trace.verify_command: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && grep -q '## RED' docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md && grep -q '## GREEN' docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md && grep -qi 'rationalization' docs/history/evolving-loop/reports/pressure-tests.md
